development.successSignal: Developers can inspect local setup and production posture from one focused desktop app
development.vision: Launchline is a focused Electron desktop app for Python environment management and production-readiness scanning.

roadmapTracks.title: Shell & Navigation
roadmapTracks.description: Core layout, navigation model, titlebar, settings, and workspace structure.
roadmapTracks.nextCheckpoint: Keep template shell stable while new apps are cloned from it

roadmapTracks.title: UI Element Library
roadmapTracks.description: Showcase pages, reusable primitives, and composed interface patterns.
roadmapTracks.nextCheckpoint: Document approved components and strengthen reuse guidance

roadmapTracks.title: Documentation & Cloning Workflow
roadmapTracks.description: Instructions for developers and AI tools to reuse the template safely.
roadmapTracks.nextCheckpoint: Create a reusable reference guide and new-app checklist

roadmapTracks.title: AI-Assisted Workflows
roadmapTracks.description: Plan how future apps can add AI search, copilots, summaries, or automations.
roadmapTracks.nextCheckpoint: Define which AI capabilities should be core versus optional

deliveryChecklist.title: Branding updated
deliveryChecklist.description: App name, initials, and shell labels match the intended product.

deliveryChecklist.title: Generic settings starter ready
deliveryChecklist.description: The cloned app has a baseline settings surface to extend.

deliveryChecklist.title: Core workflows identified
deliveryChecklist.description: The first set of real product pages is defined before implementation starts.

deliveryChecklist.title: Template guidance documented
deliveryChecklist.description: Developers and AI tools know how to reuse the UI library.

deliveryChecklist.title: Packaging and QA pass defined
deliveryChecklist.description: The app has a release checklist before shipping to users.

aiFeatures.title: Embedded assistant panel
aiFeatures.description: Context-aware helper for guidance, Q&A, and task support inside the app.

aiFeatures.title: Semantic search
aiFeatures.description: Natural-language retrieval across records, docs, or internal entities.
aiFeatures.note: Strong candidate for apps with large data surfaces

aiFeatures.title: Auto summaries
aiFeatures.description: Generate concise recaps for records, dashboards, or activity history.
aiFeatures.note: Useful when dense detail views need quick scanning

aiFeatures.title: Agentic actions
aiFeatures.description: Allow AI to trigger workflows, complete drafts, or suggest next steps.
aiFeatures.note: Add only when permissions and safety are clear

development.notes: Use this page as the project brief for apps created from the template. Keep decisions, scope, and AI plans visible here so future contributors stay aligned.

environmentRegistry.title: Primary app environment
environmentRegistry.purpose: Shared default environment for core Launchline workflows.

roadmap.title: Environment & Runtime
roadmap.description: Keep uv, Python versioning, interpreter paths, and the project virtual environment visible and reproducible.

roadmap.title: Dependencies & Capability Packs
roadmap.description: Organize reusable Python packages into pyproject.toml groups that cloned apps can enable as needed.

roadmap.title: Scripts & Tasks
roadmap.description: Expose standard uv run tasks so apps can launch processing, automation, and diagnostics from one surface.

roadmap.title: AI Tooling
roadmap.description: Plan which AI-oriented Python stacks are optional, supported, or intentionally excluded from a cloned app.

roadmap.title: Execution Logs
roadmap.description: Add reusable logs, run history, and health feedback once live execution is wired in.

capabilityPacks.title: Data & Processing
capabilityPacks.description: Structured file ingestion, cleaning, and transformation utilities for common app workflows.

capabilityPacks.title: Automation
capabilityPacks.description: Reliable background workflows, API clients, and reusable task automation helpers.

capabilityPacks.title: AI Assistant
capabilityPacks.description: Optional LLM client stack for apps that need assistants, structured extraction, or summarization.

capabilityPacks.title: Document Processing
capabilityPacks.description: Document parsing and content extraction helpers for record-heavy or review-heavy apps.

capabilityPacks.title: Analysis & ML
capabilityPacks.description: Heavier analytics and modeling dependencies best kept optional in cloned apps.

tasks.title: Sync Environment
tasks.description: Bring the local project environment into alignment with pyproject.toml and lock state.

tasks.title: Generate Seed Data
tasks.description: Generate local sample data sets when a cloned app wants seeded records or fixtures.

aiIntegrations.title: OpenAI client stack
aiIntegrations.note: Use for assistants, structured extraction, or semantic features when the cloned app needs hosted AI.

aiIntegrations.title: Embeddings & retrieval
aiIntegrations.note: Useful for semantic search, recommendations, or contextual assistants over internal records.

aiIntegrations.title: Local NLP utilities
aiIntegrations.note: Use when the app needs offline parsing, entity extraction, or language heuristics without hosted models.

secretChecklist.title: Keep secrets out of Launchline settings and roadmap notes
secretChecklist.description: Store API keys and passwords in environment variables or an external secret manager, never in the template UI state.

secretChecklist.title: Ignore local env files in git
secretChecklist.description: Make sure .env and .env.local are excluded before sharing or cloning the app.

secretChecklist.title: Use separate credentials for local, staging, and production
secretChecklist.description: Avoid reusing one key across environments so cloned apps stay safer to test.

secretChecklist.title: Review rotation and expiration before release
secretChecklist.description: Add a release step that rotates test keys and confirms production owners before shipping.

pythonTools.notes: Keep Python Tools focused on reusable app capabilities. Prefer uv-managed environments, a root pyproject.toml with grouped dependencies, and task definitions that cloned apps can keep or remove with minimal friction.
